---
# Test an IP against a delimited CIDR/IP list stored in a column

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

`incidr`'s comma-separated multi-CIDR OR works ONLY with a string LITERAL — passing a COLUMN that holds `"10.0.0.5, 8.8.8.8"` silently returns false (and the CIDR arg cannot be `"@element"`). To match an IP against a column-held list, explode it so each element is a single-CIDR scalar `incidr` accepts: `split` the column, `arrayexpand` it, then `incidr(ip, element)` per row and keep the hits. Each exploded element may be a plain IP (treated as /32) or a CIDR.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter allowed_arr = split(allowed_ips, ", ")
| arrayexpand allowed_arr
| alter hit = incidr(client_ip, allowed_arr)
| filter hit = true
| comp count() as c by username, client_ip
| fields username, client_ip
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
